content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 26, 2002

The Honorable Ross Johnson
California State Senate
Thirty-fifth Senatorial District
Post Office Box 942848
Sacramento, CA 94248-0001

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.: I-02-321

Dear Senator Johnson:

This letter is in response to your letter of November 12, 2002, to Chairman
Getman requesting advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act as they
apply to The Governor Gray Davis Committee and the Lt. Governor Gray Davis
Committee. The Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public
official's duties under the Act. (Gov. Code section 83114; regulation 18329.) Therefore,
we decline to answer the specific questions posed in your letter.

We have issued two advice letters that answer some questions regarding the
transition to contribution limits for statewide candidate committees. Fishburn Advice
Letter, No. A-02-271; Fishburn Advice Letter, No. A-02-257 (copies enclosed). We
anticipate discussing additional questions at the Commission's December 13, 2002
meeting.

If you have any questions regarding this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

Enclosures
LM:jg

Governor Gray Davis Committee. The Commission declines to provide specific advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act, citing Gov. Code section 83114; regulation 18329. The Commission has issued two advice letters addressing transition to contribution limits for statewide candidate committees: Fishburn Advice Letter, No. A-02-271; Fishburn Advice Letter, No. A-02-257 (copies enclosed). The Commission anticipates discussing additional questions at its December 13, 2002 meeting. If Senator Johnson has any questions regarding this letter, he should contact Luisa Menchaca at (916) 322-5660. Sincerely, Luisa Menchaca General Counsel
